summary: Soundboard recording captures an inspired late‑1972 set highlighted by a soaring Dark Star > Weather Prelude > Half Step and a marathon Paying in the Band.
review: The show is praised for its tight playing and standout jams, especially the expansive Dark Star sequence and the 20‑minute Paying in the Band. Reviewers note solid soundboard quality with clear instrument separation, though no major technical issues are reported. Overall the performance feels energetic and well‑mixed, making it a must‑listen for fans of this era.

ai_rating.rationale: Five enthusiastic reviews (average 4.8 stars) highlight exceptional musical moments and a good soundboard source, leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear mix with bass and vocals well‑balanced; no dropout or muffling reported.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Weather Prelude > Half Step
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set described as unbelievable and spectacular, with seamless transitions between long jams; first set solid but less inspiring to some listeners.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals on "Tomorrow" noted; overall tight playing praised.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work highlighted as nicely mixed throughout; vocal contributions on "Box of Rain" and "I Know You Rider" appreciated.
